title: Level 37.2
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 37.2: "Sunlit Pools"'''}} {{Background|file=Sunlit_Pools.jpeg}} {{Kauer Theme}} {{Headerbox|file=Sunlit_Pools.jpeg|header='''I never thought I would see something more beautiful...'''|speed=21s}} {{Class 0|security=Varying Stability|entity=Devoid of Harmful Entities|color-h=29|color-s=39%|color-l=62%}} '''Level 37.2''' is a [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Secret_Levels secret] sub-level lying between [[Level 37]] and [[Level 38]]. {{Blurimage|file=Sunlit_Pools.jpeg|caption=An image of Level 37.2, taken by a wanderer who goes by the pseudonym Jammy.}} == Overview == '''Level 37.2''', also known as the "'''Sunlit Pools'''", is an expansive complex of pristine white chambers linked together in a [[Phenomenon 14|seemingly random fashion]]. Each chamber varies in size and shape, ranging from cramped nexus areas with a plethora of doorways to vast, high-ceilinged halls containing only one or two exits, though all rooms in the level share a similar composition of white tiles that stay forever in mint condition. Similar to [[Level 37|its parent level]], a blanket of knee-deep, crystal-clear water permeates the entire sub-level, though certain areas may contain dry platforms on which wanderers may rest or dry off. In many areas of '''Level 37.2''', there are rectangular openings in the walls or ceilings through which a warm, pinkish tint shines, bathing the tiles in soft sunlight and turning the water a purplish shade of mute indigo. However, beyond these openings there seemingly only lies a white void, filled with an unknown plasmatic gas; attempts to determine the light's source via traversal into this blinding abyss have led to wanderers inexplicably vanishing, never to return. As in [[Level 37]], a slight, constant rippling effect affects the liquid surfaces across the sub-level, despite the water being otherwise undisturbed. It is unknown why this phenomenon occurs; though researchers have detected anomalies within samples of '''Level 37.2''''s water, and theorise that it may be the result of anomalous properties belonging to a certain yet unisolated element native to the Backrooms. Based on various experiments, this unknown substance appears to display properties characteristic of noble gases, and has been named ''coron'' (from the word ''corona'', referring to the outer shell of the Sun); it is expected to have an atomic number of 172, thus filling the role of the theoretical element [https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extended_periodic_table#Elements_167_to_172 unseptbium]. Additionally, its noble configuration may perhaps be responsible for the pale glow shining throughout the sub-level, as researchers have speculated that the white void surrounding '''Level 37.2''' is but a massive gas-discharge lamp filled with ionised coron, through which an electric current is passed to generate its anomalous lighting. == Entities == === Pinnicorns === '''Pinnicorns''' — from Latin ''pinna'' ("fin") — are sentient beings taking the shape of inflatable floats which can occasionally be found gently bobbing through the sub-level. These floats can come in any shape and almost any size, though they mostly choose to take the form of an equine, horned creature resembling a unicorn in popular culture. Studies have shown that '''pinnicorns''' have four inward-pointing flippers concealed underneath their bodies that they may utilise for locomotion, though they generally prefer to flow with the natural currents of '''Level 37.2'''. However, in the event that one attempts to injure one of these infatables (preferably by puncturing through the use of sharp objects), it is able to make a rapid getaway with the usage of these fins, and has been known to move at surprising speeds of tens of kilometres per hour. It is unknown what role this entity plays in '''Level 37.2'''. Some speculate that these creatures are a sort of "guardian angel", protecting the level from harm; yet others argue that they may actually comprise a secret network of cameras installed by a malicious group, in an attempt to spy on any [[M.E.G.]] operatives who may enter the level. There are others who believe that '''pinnicorns''' are simply a result of [[Phenomenon 9|tulpaefication]]; after all, there exists much circulating misinformation that inflatable pool toys are a key part of [[Level 37]]. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the presence of the [[Phenomenon 15|alone phenomenon]] here, no colonies or outposts have been established in '''Level 37.2'''. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === Gaining entry to this sub-level involves finding a shining opening in a wall on [[Level 37]], and stepping through, which will act as a seamless transition to '''Level 37.2''' once the light fades. Being a [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Secret_Levels secret] level, it is thus no surprise that this entrance is incredibly challenging to find, only appearing rarely — even more so than entryways to [[Level 399]], for example — and often tucked inconspicuously behind structures such as pillars or spiralling stairways. === Exits === Exiting '''Level 37.2''' is not as difficult as entering it. Listed below are various ways in which egress may be found: * Stepping through a dark opening on a wall, which seemingly leads into [[the Void|the abyss]], will bring one back to [[Level 37]]; * Entering a room where the light shines jarring orange instead of soft pink will send one to [[Level 38]]; * No-clipping through the flooring of more unstable sections may lead to [[Level 0.5]]; * Climbing up ladders into the ceiling which rarely appear will lead one to the underneath of a manhole cover in [[Level 11]]; and * Stepping out of any of the bright opening on the walls and ceilings theoretically leads to [[the Broken|the absolute limits of all waking realities]]. == Addendum ==
[[File:kauer.png|right|100px|link=]] '''NOTICE:''' The Kauer Thesis has a sub-theory on this level.


''[[Level 37]] was never meant to be a pool paradise.'' It was more of a paragon of refined sublimity, formed of wanderers' collective belief in the calming sensation of rippling water. Yet there are those who fear water, and they come in the thousands. Those who are afraid of unimaginable horrors lurking in the deep — their minds formed [[Level 7]], and presumably its voracious [[The Thing on Level 7|Thing]]. But the others, who despise impurities in this ubiquitous liquid? They created the [[Hydrolitis Plague|hydrolitis plague]]. [[Level 37|The Poolrooms]] was never the same again. Though the waters were sublime and the tiles pristine and polished, the looming threat of the bacterium lingered in the backs of all wanderers' minds, and they were never at rest whilst they roamed this level. But the Mandela effect spreads far and wide, and soon many began to adopt the belief that [[Level 37|the Poolrooms]] were a watery paradise, where inflatables aimlessly bobbed up and down in the ripples, and gentle beams of sunlight cast a tint over the uniform white tiles. Those who had lived through [[Hydrolitis Plague|the plague]] scoffed at these naïve dreamers. There could never be a truly perfect aquatic paradise in the Backrooms, for whatever the beliefs of water-lovers created, the nightmares of the water-fearers formed as well. And this notion was true enough, even beyond the bounds of [[Level 37]]. In [[Level 71]], [[Hydrolitis Plague|the plague]] wafts through the air in noxious yet invisible clusters; in certain areas of [[Level 330]], the lukewarm waters propagate the cultivation of the bacterium; and even in [[Level 989]], if it were not for the [[Hydrolitis Plague|hydrolitis plague]]'s natural predator of ''A. clarifex'', the whole level would be overrun with the disease. So the realists continued to laugh scornfully at the dreamers, until, of course, '''Level 37.2''' was discovered. Beforehand, all sub-levels of [[Level 37]] were [[Level 37.1|dark and dangerous]], [[Level 37.9|polluted and uninhabitable]] — yet it seemed that the collective beliefs of those who did not understand the true perils of the pools had congealed to form a ''new'' level, one far safer than any watery false paradise that had been seen heretofore. For, there was not a trace of the [[Hydrolitis Plague|hydrolitis plague]] here. As for ''why'' the bacterium is inexplicably absent, though, researchers have yet found no leads. But perhaps, ''just'' perhaps... ''...Those whose fear was fed upon to manifest [[Hydrolitis Plague|the plague]] are finally outnumbered, by the dreamers whose minds built this idyllic complex of '''Sunlit Pools'''.''
